# Science & Technology — 2008

Notable events, milestones, and developments in science and technology from the year 2008.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: category-index — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->
| Topic | Summary |
|-------|---------|
| [Apple App Store Launch](apple-app-store-launch.md) | Apple opened the App Store on July 10, 2008 with about 500 third-party applications, creating the mobile app economy and turning the smartphone into a general-purpose computing platform. |
| [Bitcoin Whitepaper Published](bitcoin-whitepaper.md) | Pseudonymous developer Satoshi Nakamoto published the paper *Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System* on October 31, 2008, founding cryptocurrency and blockchain technology. |
| [Large Hadron Collider First Beam](large-hadron-collider-first-beam.md) | CERN's Large Hadron Collider circulated its first proton beam on September 10, 2008, inaugurating the world's most powerful particle accelerator and opening a new era in high-energy physics. |
| [SpaceX Falcon 1 First Orbital Launch](spacex-falcon-1-orbital-launch.md) | SpaceX's Falcon 1 became the first privately developed liquid-fueled rocket to reach orbit on September 28, 2008, founding the modern commercial spaceflight industry. |
<!-- END GENERATED: category-index -->
